author: doll-agent
content:
Done. masked_unary_op (the hot path used by ma::sin/cos/tan/asin/acos/atan/sinh/cosh/tanh/exp/log/log2/log10/sqrt/abs and the *_domain wrappers) was a single branchy iterator pass that the compiler couldn't auto-vectorise because of the mask-test in the inner loop. Split it into two passes:

1. Apply f to ma.data() across all elements unconditionally → auto-vectorisable, gives SIMD on the bulk via the same pattern that ferray-ufunc's binary ops now use after #88.
2. Walk the result + mask, patching masked positions with fill_value.

NumPy's masked ufuncs take the same shape — wasted compute on masked elements (typically a small fraction of the array) is the cost of unlocking SIMD on the unmasked bulk. For partial-domain ops (log, sqrt, arcsin) the *_domain helpers in ufunc_support.rs validate inputs separately before reaching this point, so masked-but-invalid underlying data is overwritten with fill_value before it surfaces.

The previously-orphaned ferray-ufunc Cargo dep (already removed earlier per #272) is not reintroduced — staying in pure ferray-core land keeps the layering clean. The SIMD wins come from compiler auto-vectorisation of the now-unbranchy inner loop, not from delegating to a different crate.

199 ferray-ma tests pass; cargo clippy clean.
